title: Mind block, proc, and lambda semantics
description: Choose the right callable because procs and lambdas differ in arity checking and what return does.
body: Use when passing or storing a callable. Do not assume a proc behaves like a lambda. Actions: use a block with yield/&block for the common case; use lambda (->) when you need strict arity checking and a return that returns only from the lambda; use Proc.new/proc only when you intend lenient arity and aware that a bare return inside it returns from the enclosing method. Validation signals include a test asserting the callable returns the expected value and that arity mismatches are handled as intended. Risk boundary: a return inside a non-lambda proc can unexpectedly return from the surrounding method, causing control-flow bugs; pick lambda when return semantics matter.
